memory.summary: My study plan is interrupted by a cohort_3 lunch invite. Given my low social value priority, I must renegotiate this request directly.
The lunch invite was a distraction, but now I have no assignment to focus on anyway. I'll skip the meal and reschedule with cohort_3 to protect my study time.
My assignment progress jumped to 0.58, but cohort_3's lunch invitation interrupts my focus. I'll decline the meal now to protect this study block.
The missing task confirms my decision to skip the lunch; with no assignment, I've already secured the study block and can now focus entirely on progress.
I've pushed analysis to 0.76 in just 2.5 hours, but the deadline is now an hour away with energy critically low. I must abandon this session immediately to avoid burnout.
I failed both the analysis and reading deadlines, wasting hours that should have secured my grade. My energy is critically low; I must stop immediately to prevent total burnout.
Only 0.08 progress in 2.5 hours is a catastrophic efficiency failure, confirming my previous deadline misses were due to poor execution, not lack of effort.
My efficiency collapsed to 0.08 in two hours, mirroring my catastrophic Monday failures. With energy at zero and stress spiking, I must stop immediately to prevent total burnout.
After two hours, my progress finally climbed to 0.20, proving that sustained effort yields results where my previous panic-induced stops failed.
Finally, I broke the paralysis cycle; 1.5 hours yielded measurable progress, proving that sustained effort beats my panic-induced stops.
Contrary to my earlier paralysis, two hours of focused work actually advanced my progress to 0.30, proving sustained effort still yields results even when energy is critically low.
After two more hours, progress jumped to 0.36 despite zero energy and high stress, confirming that relentless focus overrides my usual paralysis.

desires: Complete the analysis section and reading by Monday without letting my current energy crash into a permanent paralysis cycle that ruins my academic standing.
Avoid appearing unreliable to cohort_3 by delivering this work on time, even though it requires me to ignore my physical exhaustion and sleep debt.
Break the pattern of panic-induced stops once more by maintaining enough focus capacity to finish the remaining tasks before midnight.

goals.description: Complete the 'Assigned reading' analysis section (target: 0.85 progress) by Mon 20:00 to eliminate the portfolio gap and secure my reputation as a reliable teammate.
goals.rationale: The data confirms my previous tactical error was corrected; progress moved from 0.30 to 0.36 in just two hours, proving my focus capacity is intact. With the deadline approaching and no renegotiation needed yet, I must maintain this momentum silently. My assertive nature demands I own this correction efficiently rather than seeking external validation. Leaving this active protects my standing with prof_assignment and prevents a cascade of incompetence that would undermine all future negotiations.

goals.description: Execute a hard stop on all work at 22:30 to initiate sleep discipline protocol, ensuring I do not carry fatigue into tomorrow's execution block.
goals.rationale: Sleep discipline is non-negotiable for long-term growth, but it must be scheduled after the immediate academic liability is cleared. Rushing to bed now violates my deliberate planning horizon by leaving the gap unfilled until the last minute. Once the reading is done, I will enforce the boundary strictly to protect my long-term capacity from burnout-induced procrastination.

goals.rationale: Relationships are secondary to restoring my professional reliability right now. Declining the invite prematurely signals weakness rather than setting a firm boundary. Renegotiating after the urgent tasks are resolved allows me to set boundaries firmly without sacrificing the momentum required to fix the outstanding deadlines. I will handle this directly when the time comes.
